title: Mass. family, Worcester woman identified as victims in deadly Virginia bus crash
author: Morgan Rousseau
bullets: Four members of a Greenfield family and a Worcester woman died in a charter bus crash in Virginia on May 29.
The victims were Dmitri, Ecaterina, Emily, and Mark Doncev from Greenfield, and Priscilla R. Mafalda from Worcester.
Bus driver Jing S. Dong charged with involuntary manslaughter; bus struck vehicles on Interstate 81 near Lexington, VA.

title: Connor Wong’s 3-RBI night helped power Red Sox win, and it couldn’t come at a better time for him individually
author: Colin McCarthy
bullets: Connor Wong contributed 3 RBIs in the Red Sox's win, aiding his chances for more playing time.
Wong's sixth-inning double initially called a home run was overturned but still earned an RBI.
Mickey Gasper is named primary catcher, as Wong competes to be the secondary catcher.

title: Sonny Gray continues to be great addition to Red Sox rotation, earns 6th win vs. Guardians
author: Kaley Brown
bullets: Sonny Gray pitched six innings in a 9-1 win against the Cleveland Guardians, earning his sixth victory of the season.
Gray's performance included seven strikeouts, four hits, three walks, and one run allowed, lowering his ERA to 3.06.
The 36-year-old pitcher is a key asset for the Red Sox, boasting a 2.00 ERA in five starts during May.

title: Here’s the full lineup of athletes and celebrities involved in Drake Maye’s charity softball game
author: Colin McCarthy
bullets: Drake Maye's charity softball game takes place Sunday at Polar Park in Worcester, Massachusetts.
Participants include Patriots' Mike Vrabel and Josh McDaniels, Julian Edelman, and Zdeno Chara.
The event raises funds for Maye's MayeDay foundation supporting children and families facing hardship.

title: Potential A.J. Brown Patriots-Eagles trade reportedly has ‘framework in place’
author: Kaley Brown
bullets: The New England Patriots are reportedly close to acquiring wide receiver A.J. Brown from the Philadelphia Eagles.
A trade is expected to be finalized after June 1 to minimize salary cap implications for the Eagles.
Uncertainty remains as the Patriots are reportedly unwilling to trade a first-round pick, stalling progress.

title: Did you hear it? Here’s what we know about a big boom heard around Mass.
author: Heather Alterisio
bullets: Residents in Greater Boston reported hearing a loud boom on Saturday afternoon, prompting widespread speculation on its origin.
Meteorologists identified the boom as a sonic boom caused by a bolide meteor re-entering the atmosphere east of Boston.
No earthquakes or explosions were reported, and emergency services received numerous 911 calls without finding any ongoing dangers.

title: Brayan Bello excels in bulk relief, but opener melts in Red Sox’ 4-3 loss to Guardians
author: Kaley Brown
bullets: Tyler Samaniego allowed four runs in his opener debut, leading to a 4-3 Red Sox loss to the Guardians.
Brayan Bello excelled in relief, pitching seven scoreless innings with five strikeouts and no walks.
Red Sox fall to 23-33, last in AL East, after losing six of their last seven games.

title: Giannis Antetokounmpo praises TD Garden environment amid trade rumors
author: Colin McCarthy
bullets: Giannis Antetokounmpo praised Boston Celtics fans and the TD Garden environment during a recent livestream amid trade rumors.
The Boston Celtics and Miami Heat are reportedly the top contenders to acquire Antetokounmpo from the Milwaukee Bucks.
Speculation involves the potential trade of Jaylen Brown to facilitate Antetokounmpo's move to Boston, possibly in a three-team deal.

title: Harvard ‘Senior Sunrise’ cut short by cops
author: Morgan Rousseau
bullets: Police ended Harvard's Senior Sunrise event on Weeks Footbridge due to crowd congestion and safety concerns.
Massachusetts State Police responded at 5 a.m. as attendees blocked the pathway and attempted to swim in Charles River.
Cambridge and Harvard University Police assisted with clearing the bridge; incident occurred after Class of 2026's commencement.

title: 5 must-watch movies & TV shows streaming right now
author: Kevin Slane
bullets: 'The Four Seasons' Season 2, starring Tina Fey, streams on Netflix, offering a softer tone than its debut season.
'Kill Bill: The Whole Bloody Affair' is available on Peacock, showcasing Quentin Tarantino's full cinematic vision.
'Spider-Noir,' starring Nicolas Cage as a 1930s Spider-Man, streams on Prime Video with color options.
